weather.summary: Saturday: 10°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 12°C, light rain

metadata.greeting: This weekend offers a mix of indoor and outdoor activities perfect for a 3-year-old in Hackney! From exploring the wonders of the Young V&A museum to enjoying a spring fair, there's plenty to spark curiosity and create fun memories. Remember to pack a raincoat for Sunday!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Explore the world of creativity and design through interactive exhibits. The Young V&A (formerly the V&A Museum of Childhood) is packed with toys, games, and displays to spark imagination.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits, open space, and engaging displays perfect for a 3-year-old's curiosity.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 26, 55 to Bethnal Green. Tube: Central Line to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: Check for drop-in workshops on the day.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Meet farm animals, explore gardens, and enjoy a slice of the countryside in the heart of Hackney.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, outdoor space, and hands-on experiences with nature.

transport.fromHackney: Walk, cycle, or bus to Hackney City Farm.
transport.tip: Check for feeding times to get up close with the animals.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Toddler-friendly design, space to run, and imaginative play structures.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Central Line to Stratford. Bus: Various routes to Stratford.
transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the Olympic Park.

activities.title: 'Driving' the DLR
activities.description: Ride the driverless DLR and sit at the front to pretend you're driving the train!
activities.whyItWorks: Exciting for toddlers, offers great views, and a fun way to travel.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then connect to the DLR.
transport.tip: Start at Bank or Tower Gateway for the full experience.

activities.title: Find the Elephant Sculptures
activities.description: Wander through Spitalfields Market and spot the colorful elephant sculptures.
activities.whyItWorks: Eye-catching art, open space, and the chance to explore a vibrant market.

transport.fromHackney: Bus: 26, 55 to Spitalfields. Tube: Central Line to Liverpool Street.
transport.tip: Look out for free samples from market vendors.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Explore the vast parklands, playgrounds, and walking trails. The Tumbling Bay playground is great for adventurous kids, and the Pleasure Gardens offer calmer play.
activities.whyItWorks: Wide open spaces, varied play areas, and opportunities for active fun.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Central Line to Stratford. Bus: Various routes to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the park's website for special events.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: East London's most famous park offering playgrounds, open spaces and activities for little ones.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space to run around, several playgrounds and the weather is expected to be overcast which isn't bad for a park day.

transport.fromHackney: Several bus routes available
transport.tip: Check the park's Facebook page for daily activity schedules

activities.title: Hackney Easter Egg Hunt
activities.address: London Fields and surrounding Hackney parks
activities.description: Check local community centers, parks, churches and libraries for listings of low-cost Easter trails and egg hunts
activities.whyItWorks: A perfect way to spend an Easter weekend is by having egg hunts. It is low cost and fun for the whole family

transport.fromHackney: Check local listings
transport.tip: Check local community listings

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, fascinating exhibits, and hands-on displays at one of London's most popular museums.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs, interactive exhibits, and wide open spaces to explore.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit early to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover interactive exhibits, space exploration, and hands-on science fun.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive displays, engaging exhibits, and something for every interest.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check for special shows and demonstrations.

activities.title: V&A Museum
activities.description: Explore art and design from around the world, with family activity backpacks and hands-on discovery areas.
activities.whyItWorks: Beautiful displays, creative exhibits, and activities designed for children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Green Park, then Piccadilly Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Borrow an activity backpack for a guided tour.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Discover the history of London's port, with child-friendly exhibits and hands-on activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive displays, engaging exhibits, and a dedicated children's gallery.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Canary Wharf.
transport.tip: Check for family events and workshops.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Explore maritime history, with hands-on exhibits and a dedicated children's gallery.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive displays, engaging exhibits, and a role-play space for kids.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Greenwich, then walk through Greenwich Park.
transport.tip: Visit on a Saturday for 'Character Encounters'.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.address: Great Russell Street, Bloomsbury, London, WC1B 3DG
activities.description: The British Museum is one of London's most iconic and most visited museums and is a great free museum for families to enjoy
activities.whyItWorks: It offers activity backpacks which includes puzzles, games and activities for the little ones to get involved with

transport.fromHackney: Take the overground to Highbury and Islington station, then get on the Victoria line to Euston, then get on the Northern Line to Tottenham Court Road station. The museum is a short walk from the station
transport.tip: Plan your visit in advance and check for family trails to make the most of your visit

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Soar over London with your kids at the spectacular free Sky Garden. London's highest public garden looks out over a 360° view of the city and it's a breath of fresh air that's absolutely free
activities.whyItWorks: The Sky Garden is a popular spot. The spaces are limited and you might have to wait up to three weeks but the views are spectacular and it is worth it

transport.fromHackney: Take the overground to Shoreditch High Street station. From the station, take the bus 26,48,55, 242 to Monument Station and the Sky Garden is a short walk from there
transport.tip: Booking in advance is essential

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: Disney's award-winning musical with amazing costumes and memorable songs.
activities.whyItWorks: Visually stunning, well-known songs, and a classic story.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Oxford Circus, then Piccadilly Line to Covent Garden.
transport.tip: Book in advance for best seats.

activities.title: Matilda The Musical
activities.description: An empowering musical based on the beloved Roald Dahl book.
activities.whyItWorks: Catchy songs, amazing sets, and a great story about standing up for yourself.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Tottenham Court Road.
transport.tip: Check for family discounts.

activities.title: Paddington The Musical
activities.description: Opened to glowing reviews and has quickly become a family favourite
activities.whyItWorks: Full of familiar faces and a great story, this show is bound to have your 3yo captivated

transport.fromHackney: Bus and tube available
transport.tip: The savoy theatre has excellent facilities for parents

activities.title: Lindt GOLD BUNNY Hunt
activities.price: Entry starts from the mid-teens, with under-5s free.
activities.description: Join in the fun as you hunt for the coveted Lindt GOLD BUNNY statues while exploring Henry VIII's gardens and historic surrounds. Once you have found all of the statues, you will be rewarded with a 10g GOLD BUNNY.
activities.whyItWorks: It's a perfect day out for the family

transport.fromHackney: Takes less than an hour to get there from Waterloo via public transport.
transport.tip: Designed for ages 3-12, it's a perfect day out for the family

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Search high and low for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the grounds. Each egg reveals a secret letter, encouraging curious minds to work together to uncover the code hidden throughout the Zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids will love hunting for eggs and will love seeing the animals

transport.fromHackney: Travel by bus and tube. Check the zoo website
transport.tip: Book tickets online

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Tulip Festival
activities.description: Head to Hampton Court this spring to see thousands of tulips lighting up the palace gardens. Hampton Court Palace bursts into colour each spring with its annual Tulip Festival, showcasing more than 100,000 tulips across the palace gardens and courtyards
activities.whyItWorks: The tulips are expected to be in full bloom during this period

transport.fromHackney: Head to Waterloo via public transport and travel to Hampton Court Palace. It takes less than an hour from Waterloo
transport.tip: Wander the Great Fountain Garden, the Knot Garden, and the Lower Orangery to discover vibrant beds, rare varieties, and unexpected displays tucked around the historic grounds

activities.title: Battersea Power Station Easter
activities.description: Battersea Power Station is the place to be this Easter, with family-friendly highlights including bumper cars, bungee trampolines, an elephant art trail from 26th March to 26th April , a Macaroni Penguin egg hunt on the 3rd , and Colour, Doodle, Draw with Anna Bruder from 7th-12th
activities.whyItWorks: A great way to spend the Easter period for the whole family. There are lots of activities scheduled

transport.fromHackney: Check the train and tube routes to Battersea Power Station
transport.tip: Check for times on the website

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Bluey at Kew
activities.description: Bluey-themed interactive activities for the whole family, including a Play Zone with 3D installations and the chance to meet the Bluey character
activities.whyItWorks: Great for the whole family to spend time together outside

transport.fromHackney: Check the overground and tube routes to Kew
transport.tip: Check the Kew Gardens website for events
